questions.question: Security in Fabric must meet the following requirements: Data engineers must have read and write access to all lakehouses including underlying files. Data analysts must only have read access to Delta tables in the gold layer and must not access bronze or silver layers. You must ensure analysts can access the gold layer lakehouse.

options.A: Add the DataAnalyst group to the Viewer role for WorkspaceA.
options.B: Share the lakehouse and grant Build reports permission.
options.C: Share the lakehouse and grant Read all SQL Endpoint data permission.
options.D: Share the lakehouse and grant Read all Apache Spark permission.

statements.statement: User3 has Viewer role in Workspace3.
User3 has Domain contributor access to Domain1.
User2 has Contributor role in Workspace3.

questions.question: Make Python libraries available by default in notebooks.

You have an Azure Event Hubs data source that contains weather data. You ingest the data from the source by using an eventstream named Eventstream1. Eventstream1 uses a lakehouse as the destination. You need to batch ingest only rows where the City attribute has a value of Kansas. The filter must be added before the destination. The solution must minimize development effort. What should you use for the data processor and filtering?

fields.name: Data processor
options: An eventstream with a custom endpoint
An eventstream with an external data source

fields.name: Filtering
options: A Filter activity in a data pipeline
A filter in a Dataflow Gen2 dataflow
fields.answer: A filter in a Dataflow Gen2 dataflow

questions.question: You have a Fabric workspace that contains an eventstream named Eventstream1. Eventstream1 processes data from a thermal sensor and stores the data in a lakehouse. You need to modify Eventstream1 to include the standard deviation of the temperature.

You have an Azure event hub that sends events with Street, Neighbourhood, Latitude, Longitude, No_Bikes, and No_Empty_Docks. You need to retain only events where Neighbourhood = Chelsea and store them in a Fabric lakehouse.
Determine whether each statement about the Delta loading pattern is true.

statements.statement: The target table will always be overwritten.
The merge operation will always run.
The loading pattern supports both full and incremental loading requirements.

questions.question: Complete the SCD Type 2 join conditions.

You need to ingest the data into Lakehouse1 without applying any transformations. The solution must trigger when a new file is added and provide the highest throughput.
